title: Provincial Nominee Program: Non-Express Entry Process Apply for permanent residence - Canada.ca
content:
Provincial Nominee Program: Non-Express Entry Process

## Apply for permanent residence
Processing times will vary based on
- the type of application
- if the application is complete
- how quickly we expect to process applications we’ve already received
- how easily we can verify your information
- how long you take to respond to any requests or concerns
Your processing time may also be affected if more people apply to immigrate to Canada than we can bring in
            each year.
Learn how processing times are calculated .
Once you’ve been nominated by a province or territory, you can apply online for permanent residence.
- Create an account or sign in You must apply online using the Permanent Residence (PR) Portal .

## Create an account or sign in
You must apply online using the Permanent Residence (PR) Portal .
If you or your representative can’t apply online and require accommodations, including for a disability, you may ask for the application package in one of these alternate formats:
- Prepare a new email In the subject line of your email, include the format you need (braille, large print or paper) the application package you want In the body of your email , copy and paste the text below. Then, fill in the missing information. Requested Format: [Braille / Large Print / Paper] Application Package: [Name of the application package or program] Preferred Language: [English / French] Delivery Method: [Email / Regular Mail in Canada] Applicant or Sponsor Information: Full Name (as shown on passport): Email Address (if requesting electronic delivery): Mailing Address (if requesting regular mail – Canada only): Please do not add extra information as it is not necessary and will not be taken into consideration to send out the alternate format.
- In the subject line of your email, include the format you need (braille, large print or paper) the application package you want
- the format you need (braille, large print or paper)
- the application package you want
- In the body of your email , copy and paste the text below. Then, fill in the missing information. Requested Format: [Braille / Large Print / Paper] Application Package: [Name of the application package or program] Preferred Language: [English / French] Delivery Method: [Email / Regular Mail in Canada] Applicant or Sponsor Information: Full Name (as shown on passport): Email Address (if requesting electronic delivery): Mailing Address (if requesting regular mail – Canada only):
- Requested Format: [Braille / Large Print / Paper]
- Application Package: [Name of the application package or program]
- Preferred Language: [English / French]
- Delivery Method: [Email / Regular Mail in Canada]
- Applicant or Sponsor Information: Full Name (as shown on passport): Email Address (if requesting electronic delivery): Mailing Address (if requesting regular mail – Canada only):
- Full Name (as shown on passport):
- Email Address (if requesting electronic delivery):
- Mailing Address (if requesting regular mail – Canada only):
Please do not add extra information as it is not necessary and will not be taken into consideration to send out the alternate format.
- Send your email to: IRCC.PRPortalALTRequest-DemandeALTPortailRP.IRCC@cic.gc.ca Note: we will only reply to requests for alternate formats. We won't reply to any other types of requests.
Send your email to: IRCC.PRPortalALTRequest-DemandeALTPortailRP.IRCC@cic.gc.ca
Note: we will only reply to requests for alternate formats. We won't reply to any other types of requests.
After we review your request, we’ll send the package in the format and delivery method you chose, with instructions on how to send your application back to IRCC.
If you need additional assistance applying with an alternate format, call 1-888-242-2100 (from inside Canada only).
If you’re a representative, find out how you can create an account and submit applications on behalf of your clients .
If you want to appoint someone to do business with us on your behalf, you must
- download a Authority to release personal information to a designated individual form [IMM 5475] (opens in a new tab) – whichever applies
- sign it digitally or by hand and get your immigration representative to do so also
- upload it with your application
An immigration representative (an immigration consultant or lawyer) can give you advice and help you with your application for a fee. But they can’t
- open a portal account on your behalf
- electronically sign the application for you
- sign in to the portal using your username and password
A representative can fill out forms and communicate with us on your behalf through their own account. They can also
- help you prepare the documents you need to upload
- answer questions about the forms
After you read the declaration, you must be the one who types your name . This is the legal requirement for your application to be considered “signed,” according to Canada’s immigration law.
If you’re having technical issues applying, contact us using the web form (opens in a new tab) .
- Select your problem.
- Under Tell us about yourself : If you don’t have a unique client identifier number , enter 1111111111. If you haven’t submitted an application yet, and don’t have an application number , enter 0000000.
- If you don’t have a unique client identifier number , enter 1111111111.
- If you haven’t submitted an application yet, and don’t have an application number , enter 0000000.
- Under Tell us about your technical issue : Specify the program you’re trying to apply under . Explain any problems that you’re having. Upload screenshots from your account that show us both the page and fields where you’re having problems, and any error messages you’re getting.
- Specify the program you’re trying to apply under .
- Explain any problems that you’re having.
- Upload screenshots from your account that show us both the page and fields where you’re having problems, and any error messages you’re getting.
- the page and fields where you’re having problems, and
- any error messages you’re getting.
- Pay your application fees Starting from $1,590 increased April 30, 2026 If  you don’t pay the full fees, we will return your application. We won’t start processing your application until you return it with the correct fees. In most cases, you’ll need to pay the following fees: Processing fees You’ll pay processing fees for you and anyone you include on your application. Right of permanent residence fee You’ll pay the right of permanent residence fee when your application is approved . Biometrics fee The biometrics fee covers the cost of collecting fingerprints and a digital photo. You’ll pay it when you submit your application . Otherwise, you may experience delays. After you pay this fee with a complete application, we’ll send you a letter confirming that you need to give your biometrics and telling you where you can go. Third party fees Depending on your situation, you may need to pay third parties for medical exams police certificates educational credential assessment Save the receipt as your proof of payment Upload a copy of your payment receipt to your online application when asked. Pay your fees

## Pay your application fees
Starting from $1,590 increased April 30, 2026
If  you don’t pay the full fees, we will return your application. We won’t start processing your application until you return it with the correct fees.
In most cases, you’ll need to pay the following fees:
You’ll pay processing fees for you and anyone you include on your application.
- You’ll pay processing fees for you and anyone you include on your application.
Right of permanent residence fee
You’ll pay the right of permanent residence fee when your application is approved .
- You’ll pay the right of permanent residence fee when your application is approved .
The biometrics fee covers the cost of collecting fingerprints and a digital photo. You’ll pay it when you submit your application . Otherwise, you may experience delays. After you pay this fee with a complete application, we’ll send you a letter confirming that you need to give your biometrics and telling you where you can go.
- The biometrics fee covers the cost of collecting fingerprints and a digital photo. You’ll pay it when you submit your application . Otherwise, you may experience delays.
- After you pay this fee with a complete application, we’ll send you a letter confirming that you need to give your biometrics and telling you where you can go.
Depending on your situation, you may need to pay third parties for medical exams police certificates educational credential assessment
- Depending on your situation, you may need to pay third parties for medical exams police certificates educational credential assessment
- educational credential assessment

### Save the receipt as your proof of payment
Upload a copy of your payment receipt to your online application when asked.
- Submit your online application Processing time ? : About 13 months Before you submit your application, make sure you answer all questions on all forms sign and upload all your forms upload the receipt for your fees upload all your supporting documents If your application is incomplete , we’ll reject it. You’ll have to fix any errors and then re-submit it. Submit your application

## Submit your online application
Processing time ? : About 13 months
Before you submit your application, make sure you
- answer all questions on all forms
- sign and upload all your forms
- upload the receipt for your fees
- upload all your supporting documents
If your application is incomplete , we’ll reject it. You’ll have to fix any errors and then re-submit it.
Print all pages in this section
